title: Why does Shinji want the destruction of others
display_title: Why does Shinji want the destruction of others
wikitext:
[]: Why does Shinji want the destruction of others?             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Thu Jul 27, 2006 9:07 am I just saw ep. 25. Here's what I undertand. The intrumentality project is a project where everyone is together. Gendo wants this so that people's fears and troubles will be no more (Like Asuka's mom being dead) b/c they will be together. Shinji does not want people to live together and become one. He wants to be alone. He also wants the destruction of others. But why? So nobody can cause anymore sadness to him? Such as killing someone he loves? Or someone bulling him? Why does Shinji want the destruction of others and to be alone?

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Thu Jul 27, 2006 9:13 am You should watch EoE at once. Anyway, Shinji wanted everyone to disappear because he believes that if all people are gone, he will no longer have to worry, to be hurt. However, that would make him lonely, so maybe it would be better if he never met people at all, because then the prospect of being together wouldn't exist, and so would the prospect of loneliness. Forget it, 25-26 are just headaches. So is EoE, but at least it's more analyazable.

[]:             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Thu Jul 27, 2006 9:16 am Thanks. What would I do without ya, LotP?

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Thu Jul 27, 2006 9:46 am Rahaquiel-2D1S wrote: Thanks. What would I do without ya, LotP?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jul 27, 2006 6:15 pm Hell is other people. Shinji wants a peaceful afterlife.

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Thu Jul 27, 2006 7:30 pm lol Shinji just has a twisted little mind. I mean if you were him, you would too!

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Thu Jul 27, 2006 11:26 pm This really isn't all that complicated... Shortly before Instrumentality, Shinji had all but lost the will to live. Once he was put in the driver's seat for Third Impact, he was forced to examine himself, a process which involved seeing various possibilities for how the world could change, ranging from him not existing, to him being alone, to all minds being one (SEELE's ideal scenario). In the end, he ended up scuking up his angst and accepting a reality that includes pain and loneliness, because that's what makes happiness special. He accepted that he can't be happy unless he accepts himself, rather than create a false reality around him with artificial happiness. Instrumentality was a "perfect reality" designed by flawed humans, and therefor could not have done anything but bad. While it's true that if everyone was of one mind, there would never be suffering or loneliness, it would also mean that people more or less do not exist once all minds have meshed into one, and without others to percieve one's self, one does not exist. So basically, Shinji realised that Instrumentality sucks, and decided to freakin' grow up and look for real happiness in a world with individuals. Putting an end to suffering does not mean creating happiness, and I think Shinji came to realise and accept that. Humans are individuals for a reason, and when man tries to play God and mess with that, he can only make things worse.

[]:             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Fri Jul 28, 2006 7:27 am So, what you mean is this: No suffering from being together does NOT equal happiness because people will not exist because they won't have an idea of what another person is like, because they are one. An example is that person A doesn't know what person B is like and etc. Shinji wants to live alone, because he feels that he can be happier if he accepts himself, himself meaning his mental life. He has had a rough time through his life, which is why if he accepts himself, he will find a true happiness. You cannot be happy while living in instrumentality if you are one because there is no pain and suffering, and thus, nothing to make happiness special.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Fri Jul 28, 2006 10:03 am Shinji didn't want to be alone. He wanted to be an individual. Humans are social beings, and it's in our nature to interact with eachother, and when we cannot interact with eachother we create an imaginary person to interact with in order to not completely loose sanity (like 'Wilson' from the Tom Hanks movie 'Castaway'). For Shinji to be able to embrace other people though, he had to first accept himself. And to do that, he had to reject and cancel instrumentality, because it was the exact opposite of embracing one's self. Shinji accepted a world where people can hurt and betray him, because that also meant people could choose to love and accept him, and that is infinately more satasfying than the almost non-existance of a hive mind. Shinji pretty much put things back to where they were before third impact, although people will argue until their blue in the face as to wether or not the rest of the human race returned, or entered a state similar to death without instrumentality. But that's not what matters-- the point is, the reality Shinji chose was the same one he'd be living in, because he accepted that he was the one that needed to change, not the world around him.

[]:             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Fri Jul 28, 2006 10:55 am I totally am so lost. Dammit, I'm thick.

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Fri Jul 28, 2006 11:03 am Rahaquiel-2D1S wrote: I totally am so lost. Dammit, I'm thick.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jul 28, 2006 12:08 pm Is there anyone here who, in an extreme of annoyance and frustration hasn't thought (or shouted) "I hate you all!" or "Go away and leaveme alone!" with the force of "Everyone just die!" at some point in their lives? Shinji just got caught on a bad day.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Fri Jul 28, 2006 1:50 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Is there anyone here who, in an extreme of annoyance and frustration hasn't thought (or shouted) "I hate you all!" or "Go away and leaveme alone!" with the force of "Everyone just die!" at some point in their lives? Shinji just got caught on a bad day.

[]:             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Fri Jul 28, 2006 4:34 pm Can someone please help me out in explaining this?

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Fri Jul 28, 2006 5:36 pm Rahaquiel-2D1S wrote: Can someone please help me out in explaining this?

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Jul 28, 2006 5:52 pm Rahaquiel-2D1S wrote: Can someone please help me out in explaining this?

[]:             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Fri Jul 28, 2006 5:53 pm Timstuff wrote: Shinji didn't want to be alone.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jul 28, 2006 6:08 pm Rahaquiel-2D1S wrote: Then how come at the end of EoE it's just him and Asuka?

[]:             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Fri Jul 28, 2006 6:19 pm So, here's what I get: Shinji wants a world where he lives as an individual because: -if you live as one, you have pseudo-happiness because you cannot feel pain which is what makes happiness. So, he rejects the project. Rei, having collected all the souls of everyone, explodes, and the souls go away too. Shinji then recreated the Earth as a world of pain so that happiness will feel better. He will meet people again, even if it means some people will then hate him, as opposed to the project where everyone is one. Asuka was the first one he wanted to meet. And she was there. EDIT: And lonliness is a kind of pain. Shinji wanted to be alone, but then meet people so that he will feel better. Some will hate him, but some will love him. And the ones who love him will make him feel even better.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Sun Jul 30, 2006 1:31 am Mr. Tines wrote: Rahaquiel-2D1S wrote: Then how come at the end of EoE it's just him and Asuka?
